description: A designed vertical advocacy/propaganda poster built on a warm full-bleed photograph of an older white woman with silver hair, wearing a cream denim jacket and beaded bracelet, leaning over a table to handwrite/sign a note with a marker; a blurred American flag and other people are visible behind her, suggesting a community meeting or town hall. Overlaid large display text in golden-yellow and white reads 'You have the power' (with a smaller 'the' tucked in) followed by white text 'to make your community SAFER'. Bottom-left carries the DHS seal and 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement'; bottom-right the URL 'ICE.gov/287g'. Style is an emotive lifestyle-photo poster with bold mixed-case yellow/white typography. Messaging technique: civic empowerment / call-to-action urging ordinary citizens to lobby their lawmakers to support local police partnerships with ICE (the 287(g) program), using a sympathetic 'everyday grandmother' figure to make the appeal warm and relatable. Target audience: general public/voters, especially older constituents, to generate grassroots political pressure.
notable_text: You have the power to make your community SAFER  ICE.gov/287g
